_doc: Single source of truth for the quasar framework test matrix. Each case declares its driver inputs (design/config/oracle/device_sources/generate_devices) and its 'kind' (oracle|smoke), which decides how a result is judged. Cells are (case x backend x os x compiler); .CI/expand_matrix.py flattens them into a GitHub Actions strategy.matrix, and run_test_case.py --case reads the SAME file so a case is declared exactly once. The CI image of a cell is ghcr.io/<owner>/quasar-ci:<os>-<backend>[-<toolkit_version>]. Backend coverage is symmetric -- every case runs on both o6 and uasdk now that the backends are at feature parity (a short per-cell exclude would capture any genuine semantic delta; there are none today). PR/nightly tiers and the UASDK-version axis are layered on top.
_default_arches_doc: Arches the PRIMARY OS (default_os) is fully tested on at PR. x86_64 is the baseline; arm64 adds the full per-case PR sweep on free native arm runners (only the PR-default uasdk version -- the one with arch images -- plus o6). alma10 is the primary platform; alma9 is reduced to a default_design smoke (see default_design.extra_cells).
_nightly_os_doc: OSes that mirror the FULL case set at nightly beyond default_os. Empty now: alma10 is the default_os (full at PR on x86_64 + arm64) and alma9 is only a default_design smoke -- there is no third EL to mirror. An entry would be a bare "almaNN" (full case set x every uasdk version) or {"os":...,"versions":[...]} to restrict versions.
_nightly_compilers_doc: Extra compiler(s) run over the FULL case set at nightly tier (compiler-portability coverage beyond the default gcc). NOT narrowed by representative_cases: a compiler can break on case-specific generated C++ (templates/headers), so every case is compiled under the alt compiler. clang on alma10/o6 -- uasdk static libs are gcc-built so clang is kept to o6 to avoid a cross-compiler-ABI variable.
_nightly_arches_doc: Extra nightly-only CPU arches on native GitHub arm64 runners (ubuntu-24.04-arm) with arch-tagged images, narrowed by representative_cases. Empty now: arm64 coverage moved to default_arches (full per-case PR sweep on default_os = alma10, both backends), so there is no separate nightly arch axis. Re-add e.g. {"arch":"arm64","os":"alma9","versions":[...]} to test arm64 on a non-primary OS.
_representative_cases_doc: Broad-surface cases re-run on the one case-INDEPENDENT axis that stays narrowed: the non-default/nightly uasdk versions (1.6.5 / 2.0.2) on default_os. That delta is SDK-level, so the full per-case sweep stays on default_os + the default uasdk version, and only this subset re-runs against the older/newer SDKs. Chosen to exercise the distinct codegen/runtime surfaces (plain + async methods, cache + source variables). NOTE: the default arches (default_arches, incl. arm64) and the alt compiler (nightly_compilers, clang) are deliberately NOT narrowed -- the primary platform and a compiler can each fail on case-specific generated code, so both keep the full case set. Remove this key (or set []) to run the full case set against every uasdk version too.

probes._doc: Toolchain-frontier probes (nightly/dispatch, unpinned latest CMake). Generalize the OPCUA-3442/3443/3444 class: cmake_frontier (ci.yml) = quasar configure on newest CMake (floor job covers 3.16); compat_standalone_frontier (ci.yml) + compat-standalone-frontier-windows (windows.yml) = standalone open62541-compat with real find_package discovery. Advisory until the compat pin >= v1.5.8, then flip enforcing (drop continue-on-error).
